behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher behavior across directory depth (recursive watch vs root-only). MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing logic; it appears when watcher config, OS events, and traversal interact. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can report nested events even when real watcher setup misses them. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely filesystem daemon behavior, with no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real daemon process watching a real temp directory tree. Create `root/subdir/new.txt` after startup and assert exactly one output artifact appears for that file path within timeout. Verify the same setup also handles a root-level file, proving recursive coverage rather than root-only handling.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The behavior text focuses on dedup policy for identical path + time window, which can be exercised as local function/object state with injected clock. MOCK-MISS: no required cross-module boundary is stated. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess requirement in wording. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requiring real watcher events, and no AI/ML or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the dedup component with a configurable window and fake clock. Submit request for `/a/b.txt`, then submit same path again before window expiry; assert second request is rejected. Advance clock beyond window and assert request is accepted again.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If implementation couples dedup strictly to watcher/event bus, raise to Integration; for this behavior wording, Unit is sufficient.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate events. REAL-INFRA default applies directly: real filesystem event semantics are required. MOCK-HIDE: fake watcher usually emits scripted events and can miss true duplicate patterns/timing. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependence, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp directory. Write one file using an editor-like pattern likely to trigger multiple events (open/write/flush/rename or chunked writes). Capture daemon processing logs/output and assert exactly one processing execution for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is OS-level directory lifecycle (inode replacement, watch invalidation, re-subscription). REAL-INFRA: requires real remove/recreate behavior while process stays alive. MOCK-HIDE: mocked watcher can falsely preserve subscription state. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML decision quality and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon continuously on real temp watched path. Delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, then place a new file. Assert daemon later processes the new file without restart and without manual reconfiguration.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on filesystem timestamp acquisition/resolution under near-simultaneous arrivals, plus scheduler/queue interaction. MOCK-MISS: cross-module handoff (watcher -> metadata/stat -> queue ordering) is central. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake timestamps can hide real OS granularity/tie behavior. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no agentic multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real filesystem, create multiple files in rapid succession with controlled creation timing, then verify processing order matches their creation timestamps (and defined tie-break rule if equal). Assert observed output order, not just enqueue intent.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem race: event arrival precedes write completion. REAL-INFRA: requires actual concurrent write/flush/close semantics and watcher timing. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed filesystem cannot faithfully reproduce partial-write visibility and event timing edges. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not involve AI/ML decisions or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon and begin writing a large file in chunks with delays (keep handle open). Confirm daemon does not process during active writes (no output/partial parse). After close/fsync, assert exactly one successful processing run occurs.
